rulings.Case Title: ANGELIQUE ROCHELLE ET AL VS. TREVOR DENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Trevor Deng And Defendants Attorney Edward Rodzewich
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 20, 2017, line 2. PLAINTIFFS ANGELIQUE ROCHELLE, BAZ ROCHELLE, ANGELIQUE ROCHELLE AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Trevor Deng And Defendants Attorney Edward Rodzewich. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Site Inspection is DENIED. Additionally, Defense experts also may not inspect the property. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Deposition of Calvin Chan is GRANTED: the deposition shall be limited to 1 hour. The documents to be produced are limited to: (a) DOCUMENTS related to when the construction work was scheduled to begin and when the work actually began; (b) Any emails in 2014 related to the intended rental of unit 778; (c) Any and all photographs or videos of unit 778 25th Avenue taken before construction began. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Deposition of Doug Wong is GRANTED. The deposition shall be limited to 1 hour. The documents to be produced are limited to: (a) DOCUMENTS related to the "disclosures" of the names of the tenants residing in SUBJECT PREMISES during the sale of SUBJECT PREMISES to DEFENDANTS; (b) DOCUMENTS exchanged between you and PLAINTIFFS from 2008 and 2014 related to SUBJECT PREMISES; (c) DOCUMENTS exchanged between you and Ruby Wong from 2008 to 2014 concerning the PLAINTIFFS. ; (d) DOCUMENTS exchanged between you and any employee of Laurel Properties from 2008 to 2014 concerning the PLAINTIFFS; (e) DOCUMENTS depicting any portion of SUBJECT PREMISES. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Deposition of William Wong is GRANTED. The deposition shall be limited to 1 hour. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Response to Request For Production of Documents Set Number Tow is GRANTED and the Defendants are Ordered to provide further verified responses by October 4, 2017. Request for sanctions is DENIED. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARIO DIAZ ET AL VS. LEXAMARK BUILDING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant Iglesia Pentecostal Alpha And Omega'S Partial Joinder In Motion For Protective Order Of Defendant Ernesto Paredes
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 20, 2017, line 3. Cross-Defendant Iglesia Pentecostal Alpha And Omega's Partial Joinder In Motion For Protective Order Of Defendant Ernesto Paredes. GRANTED. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARIO DIAZ ET AL VS. LEXAMARK BUILDING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ernesto Paredes'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 20, 2017, line 3. Defendant Ernesto Paredes' Motion For Protective Order. GRANTED. The Court finds that the Motion is timely. None of the reasons provided by the Lexmark Defendants to justify production of insurance documents beyond what has been provided are compelling. Even if the Lexamark Defendants had made a compelling argument, they have not demonstrated that they could not obtain the information in a less obtrusive manner. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 212-214 CALIFORNIA ST., LLC VS. CBD SOLUTIONS INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 20, 2017, line 8. DEFENDANTS CBD SOLUTIONS INC., CSBB, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith that notice provision under the Lease was complained with by either service of a 15 day notice or that no notice was required because of "failure occur[ed] more than once and there has been a prior notice form Owner to correct." Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED "as there is and can be no factual dispute concerning the contents of the" relevant portion of the Lease. Plaintiff quoted paragraph 25 of the Lease in its opposition. Performance Plastering v. Richmond American Homes (2007) 153 Cal. App. 4th 659. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. =(501/REQ)
